title: Amauropelta chaparensis
taxonomy: Amauropelta chaparensis is a species of fern in the family Thelychridaceae. It is native to Bolivia. The classification of Amauropelta chaparensis is within the genus Amauropelta, which is a part of the family Thelychridaceae and the order Thelychridales. The family Thelychridaceae is a group of ferns that are primarily found in South America. The order Thelychridales is a division within the class Polypodiopsida.

intro: Amauropelta chaparensis is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, native to Bolivia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Amauropelta chaparensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
